steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Use the standard circle equation
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation$$(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}.$$Here $(h,k)=(-4,9)$ and $r=7$, so $r^{2}=49$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $(x-4)^{2}+(y+9)^{2}=49$: center $(4,-9)$, radius 7 — wrong center.
  • B) $(x+4)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=49$: center $(-4,9)$, radius 7 — matches.
  • C) $(x-4)^{2}+(y+9)^{2}=7$: wrong center and radius $\sqrt{7}$.
  • D) $(x+4)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=7$: correct center but radius $\sqrt{7}$, not 7.
That confirms B is correct.
